title: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao

seo.title: Pineapple Bun Recipe: Make Authentic Bo Lo Bao at Home
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make the best homemade Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao) with our easy-to-follow recipe. Enjoy this classic Chinese bakery treat from scratch!
longTailKeywords: how to make Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao
best Pineapple Bun recipe at home
seo.h1: Authentic Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao)?
Step-by-Step Guide to Making Bo Lo Bao
seo.imageAlt: Freshly baked authentic Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao) with a golden, crumbly topping.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the sweet, crumbly goodness of a homemade Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao), a beloved Chinese bakery classic.

description: Pineapple Buns, or Bo Lo Bao, are a beloved classic from Hong Kong bakeries, famous for their distinctive sweet, crumbly topping that resembles a pineapple's skin. Despite the name, there's no pineapple in the bun itself; the magic lies in the contrast between the soft, fluffy bread and the buttery, slightly crispy crust. Enjoy these golden treats warm, perhaps with a slice of butter, for an authentic taste of Chinese comfort.

instructions.title: Prepare the Bun Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine bread flour, 30g granulated sugar, instant dry yeast, and salt. Gradually add warm milk and the beaten egg. Mix until a shaggy dough forms. Turn onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 8-10 minutes until smooth. Incorporate the 30g softened unsalted butter and continue kneading until the dough is elastic and passes the windowpane test.

instructions.title: First Proof the Dough
instructions.text: Form the dough into a ball. Place it in a lightly oiled bowl, turning once to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let it rise in a warm place for 60-90 minutes, or until doubled in size.

instructions.title: Make the Pineapple Topping
instructions.text: While the dough is proofing, prepare the topping. In a separate bowl, cream together 70g softened unsalted butter and 80g granulated sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in 1 large egg (reserve a small portion for the egg wash if needed). Sift in all-purpose flour, powdered milk (if using), and baking powder. Mix until a soft, pliable dough forms. Cover and chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.

instructions.title: Portion and Shape the Buns
instructions.text: Punch down the proofed bun dough to release air. Divide it into 8 equal portions (about 60-70g each). Roll each portion into a smooth ball. Take the chilled pineapple topping and divide it into 8 equal portions. Flatten each topping portion into a thin disc (about 3-4 inches in diameter). Place a flattened topping disc over each bun, gently pressing and shaping it to cover the top two-thirds of the bun. Use a knife to lightly score a crisscross pattern on the topping, resembling a pineapple.

instructions.title: Second Proof and Preheat Oven
instructions.text: Arrange the shaped buns on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving space between them. Cover loosely with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let them rise in a warm place for another 30-45 minutes, or until noticeably puffy. Towards the end of the second proofing, preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F).

instructions.title: Bake the Pineapple Buns
instructions.text: In a small bowl, whisk the egg yolk with 1 tablespoon of water for the egg wash. Gently brush the tops of the buns (only the topping part, avoid the exposed dough) with the egg wash. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and the buns are cooked through. Let them cool slightly on a wire rack before serving.

tips: For an extra tender crumb, use a tangzhong (water roux) method for the bun dough. Cook 1 part bread flour with 5 parts water until thick, then cool and add to the dough ingredients.
While the topping is called 'pineapple', there's no actual pineapple in it. The key is the buttery, sugary crust. For a deeper flavor, use unsalted butter in both the dough and topping.

Pineapple Buns are best enjoyed warm, right out of the oven. If storing, keep them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 days. Reheat briefly in a toaster oven for fresh crispiness.
article:
Unveiling the magic of Chinese baking, few treats capture hearts quite like the Pineapple Bun, or Bo Lo Bao (菠蘿包). This iconic pastry, a staple in Hong Kong bakeries and Chinese bakeries worldwide, offers a delightful contrast of textures and flavors. Imagine a soft, slightly sweet bread base crowned with a crumbly, golden, and sweet crust that cracks beautifully upon biting. Despite its name, there's no pineapple fruit in sight; the "pineapple" refers purely to the distinctive, criss-cross patterned topping that resembles the fruit's rough exterior. If you're looking for the ultimate Pineapple Bun Recipe that brings the authentic taste of these beloved buns into your kitchen, you've come to the right place. Get ready to embark on a culinary journey that promises aroma, texture, and pure comfort.

## What is Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao)?

The Pineapple Bun, known in Cantonese as Bo Lo Bao, is a quintessential sweet bun from Hong Kong. Its fame stems from its unique two-part structure: a light, fluffy, and slightly enriched bread dough forming the base, and a sugary, buttery crust baked on top. This crust is what gives the bun its signature "pineapple" appearance and its name. Before baking, the topping is gently scored, and as it bakes, it expands and cracks, creating a textured, golden-brown shell. The magic of Bo Lo Bao lies in this delightful textural contrast – the crisp, sweet, cookie-like topping giving way to the soft, tender bread beneath. While traditionally served plain, many enjoy them warm with a thick slice of cold butter inserted into the middle, creating the popular "Bo Lo Yau" (菠蘿油), or "pineapple bun with butter." It’s a simple pleasure that has captivated generations.

## Ingredients for Homemade Pineapple Buns

Crafting the perfect Bo Lo Bao requires attention to both the bread dough and the sweet crust. This comprehensive Pineapple Bun Recipe will guide you through each component, ensuring a delicious outcome.

For the Bread Dough:
*   3 cups (360g) all-purpose flour: Provides the structure for a soft bun.
*   1/4 cup (50g) granulated sugar: Sweetens the dough and aids in browning.
*   1 teaspoon (6g) salt: Balances the flavors.
*   2 teaspoons (6g) instant dry yeast: The leavening agent for a fluffy rise.
*   1 cup (240ml) warm milk (around 105-115°F / 40-46°C): Activates the yeast and adds moisture.
*   1 large egg: Enriches the dough and contributes to a tender crumb.
*   1/4 cup (56g) unsalted butter, softened: Adds richness and softness to the bread.

For the Pineapple Topping:
*   1/2 cup (113g) unsalted butter, softened: Provides the rich, buttery base for the crust.
*   1/2 cup (100g) granulated sugar: Sweetens the topping and helps create a crisp texture.
*   1 large egg yolk: Adds richness, color, and helps bind the topping.
*   1 1/2 cups (180g) all-purpose flour: Forms the dry base of the cookie-like crust.
*   1 tablespoon milk powder (optional, but recommended for authentic flavor): Enhances the milky, sweet notes.

For the Egg Wash:
*   1 egg yolk, beaten with 1 teaspoon water: Gives the topping a golden sheen during baking.

## Step-by-Step Guide to Making Bo Lo Bao

Following this Pineapple Bun Recipe ensures a delicious outcome, combining delicate bread with a crunchy, sweet topping.

1.  **Prepare the Bread Dough:** In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, salt, and instant yeast. In a separate bowl, whisk the warm milk and egg. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry, mixing until a shaggy dough forms. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 8-10 minutes, or until smooth and elastic. Gradually knead in the softened butter until fully incorporated and the dough is soft and pliable. Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1-1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.

2.  **Make the Pineapple Topping:** While the dough is rising, prepare the topping. In a medium bowl, cream together the softened butter and granulated sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in the egg yolk until well combined. Gradually add the all-purpose flour and milk powder (if using), mixing until a soft dough forms. Do not overmix. Wrap the topping dough in plastic wrap and chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes to firm up.

3.  **Shape the Buns:** Once the bread dough has doubled, gently punch it down to release the air. Divide the dough into 10-12 equal pieces (about 60-70g each). Roll each piece into a smooth ball and place them on a parchment-lined baking sheet, leaving space between each. Take the chilled topping dough and divide it into the same number of pieces as your bread dough. Flatten each piece of topping dough into a thin disc (about 3-4 inches in diameter). Carefully place a flattened topping disc over each bread dough ball, gently pressing down to adhere it, ensuring the topping covers most of the bun's surface, leaving a small exposed rim of bread dough at the bottom.

4.  **Score and Second Rise:** Using a small knife or dough scraper, gently score a criss-cross pattern onto the top of each bun. Be careful not to cut too deep into the bread dough. Cover the shaped buns loosely with plastic wrap or a clean kitchen towel and let them rise again in a warm place for another 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy.

5.  **Bake the Buns:** Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Just before baking, gently brush the tops of the buns with the egg wash. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and cracked, and the bread is cooked through. If the topping browns too quickly, you can loosely tent the buns with foil.

## Tips for Perfect Pineapple Buns

Achieving bakery-quality Bo Lo Bao requires a few insider tricks. Mastering this Pineapple Bun Recipe takes practice, but these tips will set you on the path to success:

*   **Don't Over-Knead:** While the bread dough needs to be elastic, over-kneading can result in tough buns. Stop when it's smooth and passes the windowpane test (a small piece can be stretched thin enough to see light through it without tearing).
*   **Chill the Topping:** A well-chilled topping dough is easier to handle and prevents it from melting too quickly when placed on the warm bread dough.
*   **Gentle Handling:** The bread dough is delicate after rising. Handle it gently when shaping to retain as much air as possible for a fluffy texture.
*   **Scoring Technique:** Score the topping gently but clearly. The cuts should be deep enough to encourage cracking but not so deep that they expose the bread dough too much during the second rise.
*   **Proofing Environment:** A warm, humid environment is ideal for rising. You can create one by placing the dough in a slightly warm (but turned off) oven with a pan of hot water on the bottom rack.
*   **Oven Temperature:** Ensure your oven is accurately preheated. An oven thermometer can be very helpful.

## Serving & Storage Suggestions

Pineapple Buns are undoubtedly best enjoyed warm, fresh from the oven, when the topping is at its crispiest and the bread at its softest. For the ultimate indulgence, serve them the traditional Hong Kong way: slice them horizontally and insert a thick slab of cold, unsalted butter. The cold butter slowly melts into the warm bread, creating an incredibly rich and satisfying experience known as "Bo Lo Yau." They pair wonderfully with a cup of Hong Kong-style milk tea or coffee.

To store leftover Pineapple Buns, place them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 1-2 days. For longer storage, you can freeze them for up to a month. To reheat, thaw if frozen, then warm them in a preheated oven at 300°F (150°C) for 5-10 minutes, or until warmed through and the topping has slightly crisped up again. Avoid microwaving, as it can make the topping soft and the bread chewy.

## Variations: Additions & Substitutions

While the classic Pineapple Bun is a star in itself, its versatility allows for exciting variations. Experimenting with a basic Pineapple Bun Recipe can lead to new favorites:

*   **Fillings:** Elevate your Bo Lo Bao by adding a filling before the second rise. Popular options include sweet custard cream, red bean paste, or even savory BBQ pork (char siu) for a delightful sweet-and-savory contrast. Simply flatten the dough ball, place a tablespoon of filling in the center, and carefully seal it before shaping into a ball.
*   **Topping Flavor:** For a twist on the topping, consider adding a teaspoon of cocoa powder for a chocolatey crust, or a tablespoon of almond flour for a subtle nutty flavor. A touch of pandan extract can also give it a unique aromatic twist.
*   **Dough Enhancements:** For an even softer, more pillowy bread, incorporate the tangzhong (water roux) method into your bread dough preparation. This involves cooking a small portion of flour and water into a paste before adding it to the main dough, increasing moisture retention.
*   **Vegan Options:** With the increasing availability of plant-based ingredients, you can adapt this recipe. Use plant-based milk and butter, and egg replacers for the dough and topping to create a delicious vegan Pineapple Bun.

Making your own Pineapple Buns at home is a rewarding experience that fills your kitchen with the irresistible aroma of a Chinese bakery. This detailed Pineapple Bun Recipe provides all the guidance you need to create these iconic treats, from their soft, fluffy interior to their signature crunchy, sweet topping. Whether enjoyed plain, with butter, or with a delicious filling, Bo Lo Bao is a testament to the simple yet profound pleasures of Chinese baking. We hope you enjoy trying this classic Pineapple Bun Recipe and sharing its deliciousness with loved ones!
